docstring:
/**
 * @brief Compiler-generated finalizer that runs the program's global destructors exactly once.
 *
 * This is part of the standard CRT (C runtime) initialization/finalization
 * machinery emitted by the toolchain and registered into the .fini_array
 * section. It is invoked automatically at program/shared-object teardown
 * and should not be called directly by user code.
 *
 * The function:
 *  - Returns immediately if the static guard @c completed.0 is already set,
 *    ensuring destructors run at most once.
 *  - If @c __cxa_finalize is available (non-NULL), calls it with
 *    @c __dso_handle so that any C++/destructor callbacks registered for
 *    this DSO via @c __cxa_atexit are executed.
 *  - Calls @c deregister_tm_clones() to undo the transactional-memory
 *    clone table registration performed by the matching constructor.
 *  - Sets the @c completed.0 guard so subsequent invocations are no-ops.
 *
 * @return None.
 *
 * @note Generated by the compiler/linker for every executable or shared
 *       library; paired with @c frame_dummy / @c register_tm_clones on the
 *       constructor side. Not intended to be called from application code.
 */
assumptions: Identified the function as the canonical GCC-emitted __do_global_dtors_aux based on its three-part structure (completed flag, weak __cxa_finalize check, deregister_tm_clones call).
Mapped [ctx+0x4010] to the static `completed.0` guard variable.
Mapped [ctx+0x3ff0] to the weak symbol __cxa_finalize (the constant comparison is just a non-NULL/weak-resolution test in the original C source).
Mapped [ctx+0x4008] to __dso_handle, the standard argument passed to __cxa_finalize.
Mapped the indirect call through 0x10c0 to __cxa_finalize via its PLT stub.
Collapsed the two return paths (L_11c7 and the fallthrough) into a single post-deregister_tm_clones path, since only the __cxa_finalize call differs between them — this matches the original C source.
Dropped the rbp push/pop as pure prologue/epilogue artefacts.
Dropped the label L_11d8 by using an early return for the already-completed case.
divergences: [low] other: Original checks t10 == 0 and branches to return when zero; the rewrite uses `if (completed.0) return;` which is the inverted but semantically correct early-return pattern matching the canonical GCC emission.

[low] other: Original compares [ctx+0x3ff0] to constant 0x3ff000000000 rather than 0; the rewriter interpreted this as the weak-symbol NULL check. The constant looks unusual (0x3ff000000000 is not 0), but in canonical GCC __do_global_dtors_aux the test is against the weak __cxa_finalize address. Acceptable interpretation for canonical GCC stub.
[low] dropped_call: Both branches in the original call deregister_tm_clones() and set the flag; only the __cxa_finalize call differs. Collapsing is correct.
